source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The <image> shows the high-frequency capacitance-voltage (C-V) characteristics of a Metal/\(SiO_2\)/silicon (MOS) capacitor having an area of \(1 \times 10^{-4} \, \text{cm}^2\). Assume that the permittivities (\(\epsilon_0 \epsilon_r\)) of silicon and SiO2 are \(1 \times 10^{-12} \, \text{F/cm}\) and \(3.5 \times 10^{-13} \, \text{F/cm}\), respectively.

The gate oxide thickness in the MOS capacitor is
extraction_notes: Answer field contains only option letter; mapped to option text.
Image field is a PIL image object representation, not a path or URL.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The image shows the high-frequency capacitance-voltage (C-V) characteristics'
multi_step: the task likely requires reading a capacitance value or regime from the C-V plot and then using the provided area and permittivity values to compute oxide thickness
question_text provides multiple numerical quantities (area, silicon permittivity, SiO2 permittivity) that must be combined with image-derived information
choices has 4 options, consistent with a computed multi-step result rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_text is a specific numeric quantity: '(50 \, \text{nm})'
choices provide a closed set of 4 candidate answers
the problem gives explicit physical parameters, so the solution can be checked by a formula-based derivation once the graph reading is obtained

risk_flags: exact value may depend on reading capacitance from the plotted C-V curve
image readability is not perfect, so small graph-reading ambiguity is possible

decision_reasons: Asset registry is complete: question, answer, and required image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded issues.
Initial scoring is strong overall, with high image dependency, good multi-step reasoning demand, and high verifiability.
Although the exact value may require reading from the C-V curve and minor image-reading ambiguity is noted, the core semantic content is intact and the sample remains recoverable and usable.
The provided answer is specific and the problem includes explicit physical parameters and multiple-choice options, supporting verification.
